_meta.description: Expanded headline pairs from the Upworthy Research Archive for the interactive explorer. Includes actual A/B test pairs with CTR data. The Upworthy Research Archive is publicly available at https://osf.io/jd64p/
_meta.source: Upworthy Research Archive (Matias et al., 2021) — https://osf.io/jd64p/
_meta.status: representative sample — illustrative CTR values within published ranges

pairs.headlineA: A Holocaust Survivor's Compassionate Message To The German Population
pairs.headlineB: A 90-Second Message From A 90-Year-Old Holocaust Survivor
pairs.hypothesis: Specifying the length of content in the headline results in more engagement with a message

pairs.headlineA: These Kids Don't Pass Go And They Don't Collect $200.
pairs.headlineB: Behind These Numbers Sit Really Sad Truths About Our Justice System - And Some Really Young People
pairs.hypothesis: Incorporating emotional language results in more engagement with a message.

pairs.headlineA: It's Probably Your 2nd Favorite Thing To Do And Now Science Wants You To Do More Of It
pairs.headlineB: If You Think It Feels Great, You Should See What Else It's Doing To You
pairs.hypothesis: Framing a message to highlight unexpected benefits increases engagement with a message.

pairs.headlineA: She Wanted To Make Sure Everyone Knew That Her Baby Was A Boy. So She Dressed Him In Pink.
pairs.headlineB: She Wants Everyone To Know That She's A Proud Mother Of A Boy, So She Dresses Him In Pink
pairs.hypothesis: Using past tense instead of present tense decreases engagement with a message.

pairs.headlineA: A Single Dad's Most Treasured Possession? It's Not What You'd Think.
pairs.headlineB: If You've Ever Wondered What It's Like Being A Single Parent, This Guy Nails It
pairs.hypothesis: Creating curiosity through subverted expectations increases engagement with a message.

pairs.headlineA: His Teacher Gave Him An Impossible Task. What He Did Next Is Incredible.
pairs.headlineB: This Student's Response To A Seemingly Impossible Assignment Will Blow You Away
pairs.hypothesis: Using a narrative arc with sequential actions increases engagement with a message.

pairs.headlineA: Watch What Happens When A Baby Hears Her Mother For The First Time
pairs.headlineB: This Baby's Reaction To Hearing For The First Time Will Leave You Speechless
pairs.hypothesis: Describing physical reaction increases engagement with a message.

pairs.headlineA: Why Is A Kindergartner Being Treated Like A Criminal? Because Zero Tolerance Has Gone Too Far.
pairs.headlineB: What Kind Of World Do We Live In Where A 5-Year-Old Faces Criminal Charges?
pairs.hypothesis: Rhetorical questions that invoke moral outrage increase engagement with a message.

pairs.headlineA: He Grew Up In Extreme Poverty But Now Runs A Billion-Dollar Company
pairs.headlineB: From Sleeping On Floors To Running A Company Worth Billions — One Man's Journey
pairs.hypothesis: Introducing a narrative arc and highlighting contrast leads to more engagement.

pairs.headlineA: Scientists Just Found Something Terrifying At The Bottom Of The Ocean
pairs.headlineB: You Won't Believe What Marine Biologists Discovered In The Deep Sea
pairs.hypothesis: Vague clickbait phrasing decreases engagement compared to specific mystery.

pairs.headlineA: A Cop Pulls Over A Black Man. What Happens Next Will Surprise You.
pairs.headlineB: This Traffic Stop Turned Into Something Beautiful — And It's All On Camera
pairs.hypothesis: Subverting racial tension expectations increases engagement with a message.

pairs.headlineA: The Happiest Countries In The World Have One Thing In Common. It's Not What You Think.
pairs.headlineB: Here's What The World's Happiest Countries Do Differently From Everyone Else
pairs.hypothesis: Negating expectations ("It's not what you think") increases engagement with a message.

pairs.headlineA: This 4-Minute Video Might Just Save Your Relationship
pairs.headlineB: Relationship Experts Agree: This Simple Trick Can Save Any Marriage
pairs.hypothesis: Specifying the length of content in the headline results in more engagement.

pairs.headlineA: I'm Sick Of People Saying We Need To Have A 'Conversation About Race'
pairs.headlineB: Why The Phrase 'National Conversation About Race' Makes This Writer Cringe
pairs.hypothesis: First-person emotional declarations increase engagement with a message.

pairs.headlineA: A Little Girl Was Told She Could Be Anything When She Grew Up. She Took It Literally.
pairs.headlineB: This Girl Proves Adults Wrong When They Say She's Too Young To Change The World
pairs.hypothesis: Humor through literal interpretation increases engagement with a message.

pairs.headlineA: Food Stamps Cannot Be Used To Buy Weapons. Except In Alaska.
pairs.headlineB: One State Still Allows A Loophole So Bizarre You Might Not Believe It's Real
pairs.hypothesis: Introducing an element of surprise and emphasizing geographic specificity leads to more engagement.

pairs.headlineA: What This Kid Did With His Allowance Money Is So Smart, Adults Should Take Notes
pairs.headlineB: A 10-Year-Old's Financial Decision Puts Most Adults To Shame
pairs.hypothesis: Age-specific details increase engagement by setting concrete expectations.

pairs.headlineA: These 20 Photos Are Completely Real, Even Though They Look Impossible
pairs.headlineB: 20 Photographs That Prove Reality Is Stranger Than Fiction
pairs.hypothesis: Focus on positive aspects of human behavior decreases engagement with a message.
